response:
To pause your subscription, follow these steps:
1. Click your profile icon (top‑right) and select **Settings**.
2. In Settings, go to **Billing** under the **Subscription** section.
3. Click the **Cancel Plan** button. A prompt will show the new **Pause Subscription** feature.
4. Choose a pause duration (minimum 1 month, maximum 12 months).
5. Click **Confirm Pause** to finalize. Your subscription will automatically resume after the selected period.

If you need to extend the pause later, you can return to the Billing page, click **Cancel Subscription**, select **Extend Pause**, choose a new duration, and confirm.
justification: Steps are taken from Section 1 (access Subscription Settings, click Cancel Plan, select duration) and Section 2 (confirm pause) of the Pause Subscription documentation, and extension info from Section 3.

response:
If Claude has stopped responding completely, try the following steps:
1. Make sure the Claude Desktop app is open and running for the entire session. If the app was closed or your computer went to sleep, the session may have ended. (Section 2, Claude Cowork troubleshooting)
2. Verify you haven't hit your usage limits, as reaching limits can cause requests to fail. (Section 2, Cowork usage limits)
3. Check your internet connection and ensure it is stable.
4. If you are using Claude Cowork on Windows, confirm that the Claude VM Service is running (see the “VM service not running” guidance in Section 2).
justification: Used troubleshooting guidance from Section 2 (Claude\Claude\Features And Capabilities\13345190 Get Started With Claude Cowork) which describes causes and fixes for Claude stopping work, including ensuring the Desktop app stays open and checking usage limits.

response:
Yes, you can update the name on your certificate. You are allowed to change it only once per account, and the change will apply to all your certificates. To update the name, follow these steps:
1. Open your certificate page.
2. In the **Full Name** field, enter the correct name.
3. Click **Regenerate Certificate**.
4. In the confirmation dialog, click **Update Name**.
After completing these steps, your certificate will be regenerated with the corrected name.
(Note: Once you have updated the name, you cannot change it again.)
justification: Based on the Certifications FAQs section which states the name can be updated once per account and provides the exact steps to do so.

response:
To stop Claude’s crawler from accessing your site, add a robots.txt rule that disallows the relevant user‑agents. For example, to block all Anthropic bots you can use:

```
User-agent: *
Disallow: /
```

If you only want to limit the ClaudeBot crawler, specify it explicitly:

```
User-agent: ClaudeBot
Disallow: /
```

You can also use the non‑standard Crawl-delay directive to limit crawl frequency, e.g.:

```
User-agent: ClaudeBot
Crawl-delay: 1
```

Anthropic’s bots respect standard robots.txt directives and the Crawl-delay extension. Once updated, the bots will honor these rules on subsequent visits. (Source: Section 1, “Does Anthropic crawl data from the web…”)
justification: The documentation explains that Anthropic’s bots respect robots.txt and provides an example of how to block or limit ClaudeBot via robots.txt (Section 1, Claude\Privacy And Legal\8896518). Therefore we can give these instructions directly.

response: In the US Virgin Islands, merchants are allowed to set a minimum purchase amount of US $10, but only for Visa credit card transactions. This exception is permitted under the Dodd‑Frank Act and applies only to credit cards, not debit cards. If a merchant is refusing a Visa debit card or is charging a higher minimum than US $10, you should contact your Visa card issuer for assistance.
justification: Based on Section 1 (Visa\support.md) which states merchants may require a minimum of US$10 for Visa credit cards in US territories, and Section 2 (Visa_Core.md) which references this rule under the Dodd‑Frank Act.
